source.title: Usage Insights API Contract (v1)
source.author: Jada Williams
source.body:
h1. Overview

This document defines the v1 API contract for *Usage Insights* in Redwood Console.

The API provides:
* Cost/usage breakdowns attributed by *route + model* (and optional dimensions: project, env, deployment mode, region).
* “Top cost drivers” and “What changed?” insights.
* CSV export job lifecycle (async generation + signed downloads).

This is an *API standards* contract. Service-specific implementation details (warehouse tables, cache strategy, dependencies) live in the system doc.

h2. Goals

* Provide stable, predictable request/response shapes for console and external tooling.
* Support cursor-based pagination and consistent filter semantics.
* Ensure time bucket boundaries are deterministic and well-defined with time zone handling.
* Provide a robust export job model (create → poll → download) with RBAC and audit logging.
* Maintain consistent totals vs billing, with documented rounding/tolerance.

h2. Non-goals

* Fully automated optimization actions (API may include recommendations as links only).
* Arbitrary customer-defined dimensions beyond the fixed allowlist.

h2. Versioning and compatibility

* All endpoints are under */v1/*.
* Backwards compatibility policy:
  * Additive changes (new fields) are allowed without version bump.
  * Renames/removals require a new major version.
  * Enums may gain new values; clients must handle unknown enum values.

h1. Authentication and authorization

h2. AuthN

* Standard Redwood API auth: *Authorization: Bearer <token>*.
* Requests must include *X-Request-Id* (client-generated UUID) when possible; otherwise the server will generate one.

h2. AuthZ (RBAC)

Usage Insights contains cost data and must be gated.

* Read breakdowns:
  * Requires permission: *insights.cost:read* (or workspace setting that allows cost visibility).
* Create/download exports:
  * Requires permission: *insights.cost:export*.
* Shareable links (if enabled) are governed by the same permission checks at time of access.

If a workspace is configured to *Hide cost data*, endpoints return 403 even if the user can view non-cost usage metrics elsewhere.

h1. Common conventions

h2. Content types

* Requests: JSON
  * Content-Type: application/json
* Responses: JSON
  * Content-Type: application/json
* Export downloads: text/csv (via signed URL)

h2. Time handling

* All timestamps in responses are RFC 3339 in UTC (e.g., 2026-02-16T18:42:11Z).
* Requests accept a *tz* parameter (IANA time zone, e.g., America/Los_Angeles) used only to compute *bucket boundaries* and to interpret *date-only* ranges.
* If *tz* is omitted:
  * Default tz = UTC.

h3. Range semantics

Requests can specify either:
* Explicit timestamps (*start_at*, *end_at*) in RFC 3339, OR
* A relative window (*lookback*) like 1h, 24h, 7d, 30d.

Rules:
* *start_at* is inclusive.
* *end_at* is exclusive.
* If both explicit and lookback are provided, the server returns 400.

h2. Freshness semantics

Most responses include:
* *as_of*: the latest event timestamp fully included.
* *data_lag_seconds*: estimated lag from “now” to *as_of*.

Clients should render warnings when data_lag_seconds is above the UI threshold.

h2. Cardinality and filter allowlist

To keep query latency predictable, only the following dimensions are queryable and/or groupable:
* workspace_id (required)
* project_id (optional)
* environment (optional; enum: prod | staging | dev | unknown)
* deployment_mode (optional; enum: hosted | dedicated | private | unknown)
* region (optional; Redwood region string)
* route (optional; *normalized* route, see ADR)
* model (optional; stable model identifier)
* model_family (optional)
* model_version (optional; reporting bucket)

Route normalization and model bucketing MUST follow:
* ADR: *Route normalization and attribution*

h2. Standard error format

All non-2xx responses return:

{code}
{
  "error": {
    "code": "invalid_argument",
    "message": "end_at must be after start_at",
    "request_id": "3f8b3a2a-9e1f-4e6b-8c2a-3fd0fbfe7e0d"
  }
}
{code}

Common error codes:
* invalid_argument (400)
* unauthorized (401)
* forbidden (403)
* not_found (404)
* conflict (409)
* too_many_requests (429)
* internal (500)

h1. Endpoint summary

* GET /v1/usage-insights/breakdown
* GET /v1/usage-insights/top-drivers
* POST /v1/usage-insights/exports
* GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}
* POST /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/cancel

Notes:
* Export download is via a signed URL returned by GET export status when ready.
* Some installations may additionally support: GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/download-url (alias) for clarity.

h1. GET /v1/usage-insights/breakdown

Returns a paginated breakdown table grouped by one or more dimensions, with per-group cost and usage metrics.

h2. Query parameters

Required:
* workspace_id (string)

Time window (choose one):
* start_at (RFC3339)
* end_at (RFC3339)
* lookback (string: 1h | 24h | 7d | 30d)

Optional filters:
* project_id (string)
* environment (prod | staging | dev | unknown)
* deployment_mode (hosted | dedicated | private | unknown)
* region (string)
* route (string; normalized)
* model (string)
* model_family (string)
* model_version (string)

Grouping:
* group_by (repeatable query param; allowed values: route, model, model_family, model_version, region, deployment_mode, environment, project_id)
  * Default: group_by=route&group_by=model

Bucketing:
* granularity (string; enum: hour | day)
  * Default: day
* tz (IANA time zone)
  * Default: UTC

Sorting/pagination:
* order_by (string; enum: total_cost | tokens_in | tokens_out | requests | p95_latency_ms)
  * Default: total_cost
* order (asc | desc)
  * Default: desc
* page_size (int; 1–500)
  * Default: 100
* cursor (opaque string)

h2. Response shape

{code}
{
  "as_of": "2026-02-16T18:00:00Z",
  "data_lag_seconds": 420,
  "currency": "USD",
  "granularity": "day",
  "tz": "America/Los_Angeles",
  "range": {
    "start_at": "2026-02-09T08:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-16T08:00:00Z"
  },
  "group_by": ["route", "model"],
  "rows": [
    {
      "dimensions": {
        "route": "/v1/chat/completions",
        "model": "llama-3.1-70b"
      },
      "metrics": {
        "requests": 182341,
        "successful_requests": 181902,
        "errored_requests": 439,
        "retries": 1203,
        "fallback_requests": 881,
        "streaming_requests": 176210,
        "tokens_in": 934820112,
        "tokens_out": 1120938812,
        "cache_hit_tokens": 402881920,
        "cache_miss_tokens": 1532877004,
        "total_cost": 28432.17,
        "avg_latency_ms": 812.4,
        "p50_latency_ms": 691,
        "p95_latency_ms": 1612
      }
    }
  ],
  "page": {
    "next_cursor": "eyJvZmZzZXQiOjEwMH0=",
    "has_more": true
  },
  "totals": {
    "requests": 401223,
    "tokens_in": 1900012211,
    "tokens_out": 2219001123,
    "total_cost": 51210.44
  }
}
{code}

h2. Notes on fields

* dimensions: Only includes keys specified by group_by.
* retries: Count of server-side retry attempts attributable to the request lifecycle.
* fallback_requests: Number of requests that were served by a fallback policy/model after the originally selected model could not serve.
* cache_*_tokens: Token attribution follows the metering pipeline definition; for prefix/KV cache, token accounting is based on “billed tokens avoided” vs “billed tokens processed” as standardized in telemetry.

h2. Billing consistency and rounding

* total_cost is returned as a decimal number in the workspace billing currency.
* Totals are expected to reconcile with billing within tolerance:
  * Daily range: <= 0.5%
  * Hourly range: <= 1.0% (more sensitive to late events)
* The API returns *unrounded* values to 1/10000 currency unit internally; clients should display with 2 decimal places.

h1. GET /v1/usage-insights/top-drivers

Returns top cost drivers and “what changed” explanations for the selected window compared to a prior period.

h2. Query parameters

Required:
* workspace_id

Time window (choose one):
* start_at + end_at OR lookback

Optional:
* project_id, environment, deployment_mode, region
* route (if set, provides drivers within a route)
* model/model_family/model_version (if set, provides drivers within a model bucket)

Comparison:
* compare_to (string; enum: previous_period | previous_week)
  * Default: previous_period
* tz (IANA time zone; default UTC)

Pagination:
* page_size (1–200; default 50)
* cursor

h2. Response shape

{code}
{
  "as_of": "2026-02-16T18:00:00Z",
  "data_lag_seconds": 420,
  "currency": "USD",
  "range": {
    "start_at": "2026-02-09T08:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-16T08:00:00Z"
  },
  "compare_to": {
    "mode": "previous_period",
    "start_at": "2026-02-02T08:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-09T08:00:00Z"
  },
  "drivers": [
    {
      "scope": {
        "route": "/v1/chat/completions",
        "model": "llama-3.1-70b"
      },
      "rank": 1,
      "headline": "Token out increased +35% WoW",
      "impact": {
        "cost_delta": 8120.55,
        "percent_of_total_delta": 0.41
      },
      "signals": [
        {
          "name": "tokens_out",
          "delta_percent": 35.2,
          "delta_absolute": 221990112,
          "explain": "More output tokens per request increased cost; median output length shifted from 410 → 540 tokens."
        },
        {
          "name": "cache_hit_rate",
          "delta_percent": -12.1,
          "explain": "Cache hit rate decreased; more tokens were processed by the model instead of served from cache."
        }
      ],
      "recommended_actions": [
        {
          "title": "Add response length guardrails",
          "url": "https://docs.redwood.example/optimize/response-length"
        },
        {
          "title": "Review caching configuration for this route",
          "url": "https://docs.redwood.example/console/usage-insights#cache"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "page": {
    "next_cursor": null,
    "has_more": false
  }
}
{code}

h2. Explainability requirements

Top drivers MUST:
* Include a plain-language headline.
* Provide at least one measurable signal (delta) tied to user-visible metrics.
* Avoid causal claims that cannot be supported (use “correlates with” when appropriate).

h1. POST /v1/usage-insights/exports

Creates an async export job for a breakdown dataset (CSV). Exports are used for engineering and finance workflows (chargeback, BI ingestion).

h2. Request body

{code}
{
  "workspace_id": "ws_123",
  "project_id": "proj_456",
  "filters": {
    "environment": "prod",
    "deployment_mode": "hosted",
    "region": "us-east-1",
    "route": "/v1/chat/completions",
    "model_family": "llama-3.1",
    "model_version": "2026-01"
  },
  "range": {
    "start_at": "2026-02-01T00:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-16T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "tz": "UTC",
  "granularity": "day",
  "group_by": ["route", "model"],
  "format": "csv",
  "columns": "default"
}
{code}

Notes:
* filters keys are optional; unknown keys return 400.
* columns may be:
  * "default" (server-defined stable default set)
  * "all" (includes extended metrics)

h2. Response (201)

{code}
{
  "export_id": "exp_01HTY5Q7K2Y0A0N6ZK3Z0K9P0M",
  "status": "queued",
  "created_at": "2026-02-16T18:10:00Z",
  "expires_at": "2026-02-19T18:10:00Z",
  "request_id": "3f8b3a2a-9e1f-4e6b-8c2a-3fd0fbfe7e0d"
}
{code}

h2. Constraints and limits

* Max time range: 31 days (server may reject larger ranges with 400).
* Max rows (post-grouping): 2,000,000 (returns 413 or 400 with guidance).
* Export jobs are rate limited per workspace and per user.

h2. Audit logging requirements

Export creation and download MUST emit audit events (see security guidance). Minimum fields:
* actor_user_id
* workspace_id
* export_id
* requested_range
* filters hash (not raw prompt/data)
* created_at / downloaded_at
* client_ip (if available)

h1. GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}

Returns export job status, progress, and download link when ready.

h2. Response shape

{code}
{
  "export_id": "exp_01HTY5Q7K2Y0A0N6ZK3Z0K9P0M",
  "status": "ready",
  "created_at": "2026-02-16T18:10:00Z",
  "started_at": "2026-02-16T18:10:05Z",
  "completed_at": "2026-02-16T18:12:44Z",
  "expires_at": "2026-02-19T18:10:00Z",
  "format": "csv",
  "file": {
    "file_name": "usage-insights_ws-123_2026-02-01_2026-02-16_route-model_day.csv",
    "content_type": "text/csv",
    "size_bytes": 18299331
  },
  "download": {
    "url": "https://downloads.redwood.example/signed/....",
    "url_expires_at": "2026-02-16T19:12:44Z"
  },
  "failure": null
}
{code}

Status enum:
* queued
* running
* ready
* failed
* canceled

If failed:

{code}
{
  "export_id": "...",
  "status": "failed",
  "failure": {
    "code": "query_timeout",
    "message": "Export exceeded time limit for the selected range. Try reducing the range or grouping by fewer dimensions."
  }
}
{code}

h1. POST /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/cancel

Cancels an export job if it is queued or running.

h2. Response

{code}
{
  "export_id": "exp_...",
  "status": "canceled"
}
{code}

h1. Pagination standard (cursor-based)

All list endpoints in Usage Insights use cursor pagination.

Rules:
* *cursor* is opaque; clients must not parse it.
* *page_size* defaults and maximums are endpoint-specific.
* Responses include:
  * page.next_cursor (string|null)
  * page.has_more (boolean)

If the underlying dataset changes between pages (late events/backfill), the API may return slightly inconsistent ordering; clients should treat results as best-effort for interactive exploration.

h1. Filter semantics

h2. Multi-value filters

v1 supports single-valued filters for route/model dimensions. Multi-select may be added later as additive fields (e.g., routes=[...]).

h2. Route matching

* route filter matches the *normalized* route string.
* Unnormalized paths (high-cardinality) are aggregated under the sentinel value: "/__unattributed__/untemplated" when applicable.

h2. Model matching

* model is the stable Redwood model identifier used in metering events.
* model_family/model_version are reporting buckets and may map multiple model identifiers.

h1. Caching and HTTP semantics

* Breakdown and top-drivers endpoints may return:
  * Cache-Control: private, max-age=30
* Exports:
  * Status endpoints are not cached.
  * Signed download URLs are short-lived and must not be stored long-term.

h1. Security and compliance notes

* Exports are subject to retention constraints and workspace restrictions (e.g., restricted workspaces may disallow exports entirely).
* Download URLs must be signed, single-purpose, and time-bound.
* API responses must not include raw prompts, completions, or customer payloads; only aggregated usage/cost metrics.

h1. Open questions / follow-ups

* Multi-select filters for route/model in v1.1 (console UX may require it).
* Optional “shareable link” object for exports/reports (requires expanded security review).
* Standardization of retry vs replay definitions across gateway/runtime telemetry.

h1. Changelog

* 2026-02-16: Clarified tz behavior for bucket boundaries; added export cancellation endpoint; documented billing tolerance expectations.
* 2026-02-06: Added freshness fields (as_of, data_lag_seconds) to all interactive endpoints.
* 2026-01-29: Initial v1 contract published.

title: Usage Insights API Contract (v1)
preview:
h1. Overview

This document defines the v1 API contract for *Usage Insights* in Redwood Console.

The API provides:
* Cost/usage breakdowns attributed by *route + model* (and optional dimensions: project, env, deployment mode, region).
* “Top cost drivers” and “What changed?” insights.
* CSV export job lifecycle (async generation + signed downloads).

This is an *API standards* contract. Service-specific implementation details (warehouse tables, cache strategy, dependencies) live in the system doc.

h2. Goals

* Provide stable, predictable request/response shapes for console and external tooling.
* Support cursor-based pagination and consistent filter semantics.
* Ensure time bucket boundaries are deterministic and well-defined with time zone handling.
* Provide a robust export job model (create → poll → download) with RBAC and audit logging.
* Maintain consistent totals vs billing, with documented rounding/tolerance.

h2. Non-goals

* Fully automated optimization actions (API may include recommendations as links only).
* Arbitrary customer-defined dimensions beyond the fixed allowlist.

h2. Versioning and compatibility

* All endpoints are under */v1/*.
* Backwards compatibility policy:
  * Additive changes (new fields) are allowed without version bump.
  * Renames/removals require a new major version.
  * Enums may gain new values; clients must handle unknown enum values.

h1. Authentication and authorization

h2. AuthN

* Standard Redwood API auth: *Authorization: Bearer <token>*.
* Requests must include *X-Request-Id* (client-generated UUID) when possible; otherwise the server will generate one.

h2. AuthZ (RBAC)

Usage Insights contains cost data and must be gated.

* Read breakdowns:
  * Requires permission: *insights.cost:read* (or workspace setting that allows cost visibility).
* Create/download exports:
  * Requires permission: *insights.cost:export*.
* Shareable links (if enabled) are governed by the same permission checks at time of access.

If a workspace is configured to *Hide cost data*, endpoints return 403 even if the user can view non-cost usage metrics elsewhere.

h1. Common conventions

h2. Content types

* Requests: JSON
  * Content-Type: application/json
* Responses: JSON
  * Content-Type: application/json
* Export downloads: text/csv (via signed URL)

h2. Time handling

* All timestamps in responses are RFC 3339 in UTC (e.g., 2026-02-16T18:42:11Z).
* Requests accept a *tz* parameter (IANA time zone, e.g., America/Los_Angeles) used only to compute *bucket boundaries* and to interpret *date-only* ranges.
* If *tz* is omitted:
  * Default tz = UTC.

h3. Range semantics

Requests can specify either:
* Explicit timestamps (*start_at*, *end_at*) in RFC 3339, OR
* A relative window (*lookback*) like 1h, 24h, 7d, 30d.

Rules:
* *start_at* is inclusive.
* *end_at* is exclusive.
* If both explicit and lookback are provided, the server returns 400.

h2. Freshness semantics

Most responses include:
* *as_of*: the latest event timestamp fully included.
* *data_lag_seconds*: estimated lag from “now” to *as_of*.

Clients should render warnings when data_lag_seconds is above the UI threshold.

h2. Cardinality and filter allowlist

To keep query latency predictable, only the following dimensions are queryable and/or groupable:
* workspace_id (required)
* project_id (optional)
* environment (optional; enum: prod | staging | dev | unknown)
* deployment_mode (optional; enum: hosted | dedicated | private | unknown)
* region (optional; Redwood region string)
* route (optional; *normalized* route, see ADR)
* model (optional; stable model identifier)
* model_family (optional)
* model_version (optional; reporting bucket)

Route normalization and model bucketing MUST follow:
* ADR: *Route normalization and attribution*

h2. Standard error format

All non-2xx responses return:

{code}
{
  "error": {
    "code": "invalid_argument",
    "message": "end_at must be after start_at",
    "request_id": "3f8b3a2a-9e1f-4e6b-8c2a-3fd0fbfe7e0d"
  }
}
{code}

Common error codes:
* invalid_argument (400)
* unauthorized (401)
* forbidden (403)
* not_found (404)
* conflict (409)
* too_many_requests (429)
* internal (500)

h1. Endpoint summary

* GET /v1/usage-insights/breakdown
* GET /v1/usage-insights/top-drivers
* POST /v1/usage-insights/exports
* GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}
* POST /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/cancel

Notes:
* Export download is via a signed URL returned by GET export status when ready.
* Some installations may additionally support: GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/download-url (alias) for clarity.

h1. GET /v1/usage-insights/breakdown

Returns a paginated breakdown table grouped by one or more dimensions, with per-group cost and usage metrics.

h2. Query parameters

Required:
* workspace_id (string)

Time window (choose one):
* start_at (RFC3339)
* end_at (RFC3339)
* lookback (string: 1h | 24h | 7d | 30d)

Optional filters:
* project_id (string)
* environment (prod | staging | dev | unknown)
* deployment_mode (hosted | dedicated | private | unknown)
* region (string)
* route (string; normalized)
* model (string)
* model_family (string)
* model_version (string)

Grouping:
* group_by (repeatable query param; allowed values: route, model, model_family, model_version, region, deployment_mode, environment, project_id)
  * Default: group_by=route&group_by=model

Bucketing:
* granularity (string; enum: hour | day)
  * Default: day
* tz (IANA time zone)
  * Default: UTC

Sorting/pagination:
* order_by (string; enum: total_cost | tokens_in | tokens_out | requests | p95_latency_ms)
  * Default: total_cost
* order (asc | desc)
  * Default: desc
* page_size (int; 1–500)
  * Default: 100
* cursor (opaque string)

h2. Response shape

{code}
{
  "as_of": "2026-02-16T18:00:00Z",
  "data_lag_seconds": 420,
  "currency": "USD",
  "granularity": "day",
  "tz": "America/Los_Angeles",
  "range": {
    "start_at": "2026-02-09T08:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-16T08:00:00Z"
  },
  "group_by": ["route", "model"],
  "rows": [
    {
      "dimensions": {
        "route": "/v1/chat/completions",
        "model": "llama-3.1-70b"
      },
      "metrics": {
        "requests": 182341,
        "successful_requests": 181902,
        "errored_requests": 439,
        "retries": 1203,
        "fallback_requests": 881,
        "streaming_requests": 176210,
        "tokens_in": 934820112,
        "tokens_out": 1120938812,
        "cache_hit_tokens": 402881920,
        "cache_miss_tokens": 1532877004,
        "total_cost": 28432.17,
        "avg_latency_ms": 812.4,
        "p50_latency_ms": 691,
        "p95_latency_ms": 1612
      }
    }
  ],
  "page": {
    "next_cursor": "eyJvZmZzZXQiOjEwMH0=",
    "has_more": true
  },
  "totals": {
    "requests": 401223,
    "tokens_in": 1900012211,
    "tokens_out": 2219001123,
    "total_cost": 51210.44
  }
}
{code}

h2. Notes on fields

* dimensions: Only includes keys specified by group_by.
* retries: Count of server-side retry attempts attributable to the request lifecycle.
* fallback_requests: Number of requests that were served by a fallback policy/model after the originally selected model could not serve.
* cache_*_tokens: Token attribution follows the metering pipeline definition; for prefix/KV cache, token accounting is based on “billed tokens avoided” vs “billed tokens processed” as standardized in telemetry.

* total_cost is returned as a decimal number in the workspace billing currency.

  * Daily range: <= 0.5%
  * Hourly range: <= 1.0% (more sensitive to late events)
* The API returns *unrounded* values to 1/10000 currency unit internally; clients should display with 2 decimal places.

    {

text:
h1. Overview

This document defines the v1 API contract for *Usage Insights* in Redwood Console.

The API provides:
* Cost/usage breakdowns attributed by *route + model* (and optional dimensions: project, env, deployment mode, region).
* “Top cost drivers” and “What changed?” insights.
* CSV export job lifecycle (async generation + signed downloads).

This is an *API standards* contract. Service-specific implementation details (warehouse tables, cache strategy, dependencies) live in the system doc.

h2. Goals

* Provide stable, predictable request/response shapes for console and external tooling.
* Support cursor-based pagination and consistent filter semantics.
* Ensure time bucket boundaries are deterministic and well-defined with time zone handling.
* Provide a robust export job model (create → poll → download) with RBAC and audit logging.
* Maintain consistent totals vs billing, with documented rounding/tolerance.

h2. Non-goals

* Fully automated optimization actions (API may include recommendations as links only).
* Arbitrary customer-defined dimensions beyond the fixed allowlist.

h2. Versioning and compatibility

* All endpoints are under */v1/*.
* Backwards compatibility policy:
  * Additive changes (new fields) are allowed without version bump.
  * Renames/removals require a new major version.
  * Enums may gain new values; clients must handle unknown enum values.

h1. Authentication and authorization

h2. AuthN

* Standard Redwood API auth: *Authorization: Bearer <token>*.
* Requests must include *X-Request-Id* (client-generated UUID) when possible; otherwise the server will generate one.

h2. AuthZ (RBAC)

Usage Insights contains cost data and must be gated.

* Read breakdowns:
  * Requires permission: *insights.cost:read* (or workspace setting that allows cost visibility).
* Create/download exports:
  * Requires permission: *insights.cost:export*.
* Shareable links (if enabled) are governed by the same permission checks at time of access.

If a workspace is configured to *Hide cost data*, endpoints return 403 even if the user can view non-cost usage metrics elsewhere.

h1. Common conventions

h2. Content types

* Requests: JSON
  * Content-Type: application/json
* Responses: JSON
  * Content-Type: application/json
* Export downloads: text/csv (via signed URL)

h2. Time handling

* All timestamps in responses are RFC 3339 in UTC (e.g., 2026-02-16T18:42:11Z).
* Requests accept a *tz* parameter (IANA time zone, e.g., America/Los_Angeles) used only to compute *bucket boundaries* and to interpret *date-only* ranges.
* If *tz* is omitted:
  * Default tz = UTC.

h3. Range semantics

Requests can specify either:
* Explicit timestamps (*start_at*, *end_at*) in RFC 3339, OR
* A relative window (*lookback*) like 1h, 24h, 7d, 30d.

Rules:
* *start_at* is inclusive.
* *end_at* is exclusive.
* If both explicit and lookback are provided, the server returns 400.

h2. Freshness semantics

Most responses include:
* *as_of*: the latest event timestamp fully included.
* *data_lag_seconds*: estimated lag from “now” to *as_of*.

Clients should render warnings when data_lag_seconds is above the UI threshold.

h2. Cardinality and filter allowlist

To keep query latency predictable, only the following dimensions are queryable and/or groupable:
* workspace_id (required)
* project_id (optional)
* environment (optional; enum: prod | staging | dev | unknown)
* deployment_mode (optional; enum: hosted | dedicated | private | unknown)
* region (optional; Redwood region string)
* route (optional; *normalized* route, see ADR)
* model (optional; stable model identifier)
* model_family (optional)
* model_version (optional; reporting bucket)

Route normalization and model bucketing MUST follow:
* ADR: *Route normalization and attribution*

h2. Standard error format

All non-2xx responses return:

{code}
{
  "error": {
    "code": "invalid_argument",
    "message": "end_at must be after start_at",
    "request_id": "3f8b3a2a-9e1f-4e6b-8c2a-3fd0fbfe7e0d"
  }
}
{code}

Common error codes:
* invalid_argument (400)
* unauthorized (401)
* forbidden (403)
* not_found (404)
* conflict (409)
* too_many_requests (429)
* internal (500)

h1. Endpoint summary

* GET /v1/usage-insights/breakdown
* GET /v1/usage-insights/top-drivers
* POST /v1/usage-insights/exports
* GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}
* POST /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/cancel

Notes:
* Export download is via a signed URL returned by GET export status when ready.
* Some installations may additionally support: GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/download-url (alias) for clarity.

h1. GET /v1/usage-insights/breakdown

Returns a paginated breakdown table grouped by one or more dimensions, with per-group cost and usage metrics.

h2. Query parameters

Required:
* workspace_id (string)

Time window (choose one):
* start_at (RFC3339)
* end_at (RFC3339)
* lookback (string: 1h | 24h | 7d | 30d)

Optional filters:
* project_id (string)
* environment (prod | staging | dev | unknown)
* deployment_mode (hosted | dedicated | private | unknown)
* region (string)
* route (string; normalized)
* model (string)
* model_family (string)
* model_version (string)

Grouping:
* group_by (repeatable query param; allowed values: route, model, model_family, model_version, region, deployment_mode, environment, project_id)
  * Default: group_by=route&group_by=model

Bucketing:
* granularity (string; enum: hour | day)
  * Default: day
* tz (IANA time zone)
  * Default: UTC

Sorting/pagination:
* order_by (string; enum: total_cost | tokens_in | tokens_out | requests | p95_latency_ms)
  * Default: total_cost
* order (asc | desc)
  * Default: desc
* page_size (int; 1–500)
  * Default: 100
* cursor (opaque string)

h2. Response shape

{code}
{
  "as_of": "2026-02-16T18:00:00Z",
  "data_lag_seconds": 420,
  "currency": "USD",
  "granularity": "day",
  "tz": "America/Los_Angeles",
  "range": {
    "start_at": "2026-02-09T08:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-16T08:00:00Z"
  },
  "group_by": ["route", "model"],
  "rows": [
    {
      "dimensions": {
        "route": "/v1/chat/completions",
        "model": "llama-3.1-70b"
      },
      "metrics": {
        "requests": 182341,
        "successful_requests": 181902,
        "errored_requests": 439,
        "retries": 1203,
        "fallback_requests": 881,
        "streaming_requests": 176210,
        "tokens_in": 934820112,
        "tokens_out": 1120938812,
        "cache_hit_tokens": 402881920,
        "cache_miss_tokens": 1532877004,
        "total_cost": 28432.17,
        "avg_latency_ms": 812.4,
        "p50_latency_ms": 691,
        "p95_latency_ms": 1612
      }
    }
  ],
  "page": {
    "next_cursor": "eyJvZmZzZXQiOjEwMH0=",
    "has_more": true
  },
  "totals": {
    "requests": 401223,
    "tokens_in": 1900012211,
    "tokens_out": 2219001123,
    "total_cost": 51210.44
  }
}
{code}

h2. Notes on fields

* dimensions: Only includes keys specified by group_by.
* retries: Count of server-side retry attempts attributable to the request lifecycle.
* fallback_requests: Number of requests that were served by a fallback policy/model after the originally selected model could not serve.
* cache_*_tokens: Token attribution follows the metering pipeline definition; for prefix/KV cache, token accounting is based on “billed tokens avoided” vs “billed tokens processed” as standardized in telemetry.

h2. Billing consistency and rounding

* total_cost is returned as a decimal number in the workspace billing currency.
* Totals are expected to reconcile with billing within tolerance:
  * Daily range: <= 0.5%
  * Hourly range: <= 1.0% (more sensitive to late events)
* The API returns *unrounded* values to 1/10000 currency unit internally; clients should display with 2 decimal places.

h1. GET /v1/usage-insights/top-drivers

Returns top cost drivers and “what changed” explanations for the selected window compared to a prior period.

h2. Query parameters

Required:
* workspace_id

Time window (choose one):
* start_at + end_at OR lookback

Optional:
* project_id, environment, deployment_mode, region
* route (if set, provides drivers within a route)
* model/model_family/model_version (if set, provides drivers within a model bucket)

Comparison:
* compare_to (string; enum: previous_period | previous_week)
  * Default: previous_period
* tz (IANA time zone; default UTC)

Pagination:
* page_size (1–200; default 50)
* cursor

h2. Response shape

{code}
{
  "as_of": "2026-02-16T18:00:00Z",
  "data_lag_seconds": 420,
  "currency": "USD",
  "range": {
    "start_at": "2026-02-09T08:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-16T08:00:00Z"
  },
  "compare_to": {
    "mode": "previous_period",
    "start_at": "2026-02-02T08:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-09T08:00:00Z"
  },
  "drivers": [
    {
      "scope": {
        "route": "/v1/chat/completions",
        "model": "llama-3.1-70b"
      },
      "rank": 1,
      "headline": "Token out increased +35% WoW",
      "impact": {
        "cost_delta": 8120.55,
        "percent_of_total_delta": 0.41
      },
      "signals": [
        {
          "name": "tokens_out",
          "delta_percent": 35.2,
          "delta_absolute": 221990112,
          "explain": "More output tokens per request increased cost; median output length shifted from 410 → 540 tokens."
        },
        {
          "name": "cache_hit_rate",
          "delta_percent": -12.1,
          "explain": "Cache hit rate decreased; more tokens were processed by the model instead of served from cache."
        }
      ],
      "recommended_actions": [
        {
          "title": "Add response length guardrails",
          "url": "https://docs.redwood.example/optimize/response-length"
        },
        {
          "title": "Review caching configuration for this route",
          "url": "https://docs.redwood.example/console/usage-insights#cache"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "page": {
    "next_cursor": null,
    "has_more": false
  }
}
{code}

h2. Explainability requirements

Top drivers MUST:
* Include a plain-language headline.
* Provide at least one measurable signal (delta) tied to user-visible metrics.
* Avoid causal claims that cannot be supported (use “correlates with” when appropriate).

h1. POST /v1/usage-insights/exports

Creates an async export job for a breakdown dataset (CSV). Exports are used for engineering and finance workflows (chargeback, BI ingestion).

h2. Request body

{code}
{
  "workspace_id": "ws_123",
  "project_id": "proj_456",
  "filters": {
    "environment": "prod",
    "deployment_mode": "hosted",
    "region": "us-east-1",
    "route": "/v1/chat/completions",
    "model_family": "llama-3.1",
    "model_version": "2026-01"
  },
  "range": {
    "start_at": "2026-02-01T00:00:00Z",
    "end_at": "2026-02-16T00:00:00Z"
  },
  "tz": "UTC",
  "granularity": "day",
  "group_by": ["route", "model"],
  "format": "csv",
  "columns": "default"
}
{code}

Notes:
* filters keys are optional; unknown keys return 400.
* columns may be:
  * "default" (server-defined stable default set)
  * "all" (includes extended metrics)

h2. Response (201)

{code}
{
  "export_id": "exp_01HTY5Q7K2Y0A0N6ZK3Z0K9P0M",
  "status": "queued",
  "created_at": "2026-02-16T18:10:00Z",
  "expires_at": "2026-02-19T18:10:00Z",
  "request_id": "3f8b3a2a-9e1f-4e6b-8c2a-3fd0fbfe7e0d"
}
{code}

h2. Constraints and limits

* Max time range: 31 days (server may reject larger ranges with 400).
* Max rows (post-grouping): 2,000,000 (returns 413 or 400 with guidance).
* Export jobs are rate limited per workspace and per user.

h2. Audit logging requirements

Export creation and download MUST emit audit events (see security guidance). Minimum fields:
* actor_user_id
* workspace_id
* export_id
* requested_range
* filters hash (not raw prompt/data)
* created_at / downloaded_at
* client_ip (if available)

h1. GET /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}

Returns export job status, progress, and download link when ready.

h2. Response shape

{code}
{
  "export_id": "exp_01HTY5Q7K2Y0A0N6ZK3Z0K9P0M",
  "status": "ready",
  "created_at": "2026-02-16T18:10:00Z",
  "started_at": "2026-02-16T18:10:05Z",
  "completed_at": "2026-02-16T18:12:44Z",
  "expires_at": "2026-02-19T18:10:00Z",
  "format": "csv",
  "file": {
    "file_name": "usage-insights_ws-123_2026-02-01_2026-02-16_route-model_day.csv",
    "content_type": "text/csv",
    "size_bytes": 18299331
  },
  "download": {
    "url": "https://downloads.redwood.example/signed/....",
    "url_expires_at": "2026-02-16T19:12:44Z"
  },
  "failure": null
}
{code}

Status enum:
* queued
* running
* ready
* failed
* canceled

If failed:

{code}
{
  "export_id": "...",
  "status": "failed",
  "failure": {
    "code": "query_timeout",
    "message": "Export exceeded time limit for the selected range. Try reducing the range or grouping by fewer dimensions."
  }
}
{code}

h1. POST /v1/usage-insights/exports/{export_id}/cancel

Cancels an export job if it is queued or running.

h2. Response

{code}
{
  "export_id": "exp_...",
  "status": "canceled"
}
{code}

h1. Pagination standard (cursor-based)

All list endpoints in Usage Insights use cursor pagination.

Rules:
* *cursor* is opaque; clients must not parse it.
* *page_size* defaults and maximums are endpoint-specific.
* Responses include:
  * page.next_cursor (string|null)
  * page.has_more (boolean)

If the underlying dataset changes between pages (late events/backfill), the API may return slightly inconsistent ordering; clients should treat results as best-effort for interactive exploration.

h1. Filter semantics

h2. Multi-value filters

v1 supports single-valued filters for route/model dimensions. Multi-select may be added later as additive fields (e.g., routes=[...]).

h2. Route matching

* route filter matches the *normalized* route string.
* Unnormalized paths (high-cardinality) are aggregated under the sentinel value: "/__unattributed__/untemplated" when applicable.

h2. Model matching

* model is the stable Redwood model identifier used in metering events.
* model_family/model_version are reporting buckets and may map multiple model identifiers.

h1. Caching and HTTP semantics

* Breakdown and top-drivers endpoints may return:
  * Cache-Control: private, max-age=30
* Exports:
  * Status endpoints are not cached.
  * Signed download URLs are short-lived and must not be stored long-term.

h1. Security and compliance notes

* Exports are subject to retention constraints and workspace restrictions (e.g., restricted workspaces may disallow exports entirely).
* Download URLs must be signed, single-purpose, and time-bound.
* API responses must not include raw prompts, completions, or customer payloads; only aggregated usage/cost metrics.

h1. Open questions / follow-ups

* Multi-select filters for route/model in v1.1 (console UX may require it).
* Optional “shareable link” object for exports/reports (requires expanded security review).
* Standardization of retry vs replay definitions across gateway/runtime telemetry.

h1. Changelog

* 2026-02-16: Clarified tz behavior for bucket boundaries; added export cancellation endpoint; documented billing tolerance expectations.
* 2026-02-06: Added freshness fields (as_of, data_lag_seconds) to all interactive endpoints.
* 2026-01-29: Initial v1 contract published.

source.title: Perf Canary Overhead and Safety Standards
source.author: Allison Grant
source.content:
## Summary
This document defines mandatory overhead and safety standards for the production **perf-canary** service (the always-on synthetic performance canary used to detect serving-runtime regressions per model).

These standards exist to ensure:
- Canary load remains **non-disruptive** to customer workloads (bounded GPU capacity and QPS).
- Canary traffic is **isolated** (least privilege, no customer data access).
- Canary telemetry is **safe-by-default** (no prompts, no PII, controlled metric cardinality).
- The canary has clear **kill switches** and rollout controls to reduce blast radius.

## Scope
Applies to:
- The **perf-canary** service and any sidecars/jobs that generate synthetic inference traffic in production.
- Related telemetry (metrics, traces, logs) produced by canary runs.
- Control-plane configuration surfaces that enable/disable canary per region/tier/model.

Does not apply to:
- Customer-traffic shadowing or replay (explicitly out of scope for perf-canary MVP).
- Offline benchmarking jobs in staging/benchmark-lab (covered by separate performance test standards).

## Definitions
- **Covered model**: model included in canary coverage list for a region/tier.
- **Region**: Redwood production region (e.g., us-east, eu-west).
- **Tier**: capacity tier/pool class (Hosted, Dedicated, Private) or internal pool partition as defined by runtime control plane.
- **GPU overhead**: incremental GPU time/compute consumed by canary traffic, expressed as a % of provisioned capacity for the targeted pool(s).
- **Synthetic prompt**: shape-only prompt content generated by Redwood-owned templates. Must not include customer prompts or user identifiers.

## Hard requirements (must)
### R1: GPU overhead budgets
Perf-canary must remain under the following overhead budgets:
- **Per region (aggregate across all covered models): <= 0.5%** of provisioned GPU capacity for the region’s targeted pool(s).
- **Per model (within a region/tier): <= 0.10%** of provisioned GPU capacity for that model’s targeted pool(s).
- **Burst allowance (short-lived):** up to **1.0%** region aggregate for **<= 10 minutes** during controlled operations (e.g., baseline recomputation) with SRE approval and explicit change record.

Interpretation and measurement:
- Overhead is measured as GPU-seconds (or equivalent utilization proxy) attributable to canary requests vs total GPU-seconds in the targeted pool(s).
- If the canary runs across multiple SKUs in a region, overhead must be computed per SKU pool and then aggregated.

Automatic enforcement:
- perf-canary must enforce a **capacity budget** limiter. When the budget is exhausted, canary requests must back off (do not retry aggressively).
- If the overhead limiter fails closed (cannot compute budget), perf-canary must default to **safe minimum** operation (see R8).

### R2: QPS and concurrency caps
Perf-canary must enforce caps at three levels:
1) **Global (service-wide) cap** per region (default):
   - Max canary QPS (region): **0.2 QPS** per covered model, with a region-wide hard max of **2 QPS**.
2) **Per model cap** per region/tier (default):
   - Max canary QPS (model): **0.2 QPS**
   - Max in-flight requests (model): **2**
3) **Per endpoint cap** (safety net):
   - If the inference endpoint reports elevated errors/overload signals, canary must reduce QPS to **<= 0.02 QPS** and emit a “canary_throttled” metric.

Notes:
- Defaults above are intentionally conservative and can be tuned downward for small pools.
- Any increase above defaults requires SRE review + documented capacity impact estimate.

### R3: Traffic isolation and routing
Perf-canary traffic must be isolated so it does not compete unexpectedly with customer traffic:
- perf-canary must use a dedicated **service account / auth scope** ("internal.canary.inference.read") with least privilege.
- Requests must include a canary-only routing attribute (e.g., header `X-Redwood-Canary: perf`) so runtime can:
  - apply separate rate limits,
  - exclude canary traffic from customer billing/quotas,
  - optionally route to designated canary partitions when available.
- Canary traffic must not bypass normal admission control. If the system is overloaded, canary should back off.

### R4: No customer data; no prompt storage
Perf-canary must not collect, store, or emit customer data:
- Canary prompts must be **synthetic** and generated by Redwood-owned templates.
- Canary must not ingest prompts from production traffic, logs, traces, queues, dead-letter streams, or customer datasets.
- Canary must not log prompt text, completions, embeddings content, or tool/function arguments.

Allowed:
- Non-content metadata required for debugging (timestamps, model id, region, runtime build tag, kernel tag, sample counts).
- Token counts (input/output lengths) and latency/throughput measurements.

### R5: PII and secrets handling
Perf-canary must enforce and validate:
- Redaction on all logs (prompt-like fields must be dropped, not masked).
- Explicit assertions in code that prevent prompt payloads from reaching log/trace exporters.
- No API keys, bearer tokens, or customer identifiers in logs/metrics/traces.
- Service account credentials must be stored only in approved secret stores (no env var plaintext in manifests).

### R6: Metric label cardinality limits
To prevent metric explosions and alert storms:
- Metric dimensions must be limited to:
  - `model`, `model_version`, `region`, `tier`, `hardware_sku`, `runtime_build_id`, `kernel_tag` (when available), `workload_profile` (small fixed set)
- Explicitly disallowed labels:
  - `request_id`, `trace_id`, `pod`, `node`, `gpu_uuid`, `customer_id`, `prompt_hash`, any free-form string.
- `model_version` must use canonical registry IDs (not arbitrary strings).

Any new label requires telemetry platform review (Logan Wright / Nadia Rahman or delegate).

### R7: Safe defaults for traces and logs
- Tracing sampling for perf-canary must be **low by default** (e.g., 0.1–1%) and must never capture request/response content.
- Logs must be structured and limited to warnings/errors + periodic summary lines (e.g., every 5 minutes).
- Debug logging that increases verbosity must be gated behind a runtime flag and time-limited.

### R8: Kill switches and fail-safe behavior
Perf-canary must support:
- **Control-plane disable switch** per region/tier/model (audited, with last-changed metadata).
- **Emergency global disable** (single toggle) that stops request generation within **60 seconds**.

Fail-safe behavior:
- If baseline store is unavailable, telemetry is delayed, or capacity budget computation fails, canary must:
  - reduce QPS to **<= 0.02 QPS per model** (or fully disable if configured), and
  - emit a single warning alert (non-paging) indicating degraded canary mode.

### R9: Do-not-page conditions (protect on-call)
perf-canary must suppress paging in these conditions (warn-only):
- Known telemetry ingestion delays beyond threshold (e.g., >10 minutes lag).
- Canary sample size below minimum thresholds for the window.
- Ongoing region incident or declared capacity event (as signaled by SRE incident state).

### R10: Change management and exceptions
- Any configuration change that increases load (QPS/concurrency/workload size) must be documented with:
  - estimated incremental overhead (%),
  - expected affected pools (region/tier/SKU),
  - rollback plan.
- Exceptions require approval from:
  - Serving Runtime TL (Noah Patel or delegate) AND
  - SRE sponsor (Sean Gallagher or delegate)

Exceptions must be time-bounded and tracked as a follow-up task.

## Recommended targets (should)
### S1: Workload profiles and run cadence
- Use a **small fixed set** of workload profiles (e.g., `short_prefill`, `medium_chat`, `long_prefill`, `decode_heavy`) to avoid config drift.
- Run cadence should be steady (avoid synchronized spikes at minute boundaries). Use jitter (e.g., +/- 20%).

### S2: Pool-aware backoff
- If runtime reports elevated queueing time or overload signals, canary should:
  - reduce QPS,
  - increase jitter,
  - optionally skip the heaviest prompt bucket.

### S3: Baseline recomputation discipline
- Baseline recomputation (if done in production-like environments) should be scheduled during lower-risk windows and must honor burst allowance rules.

## Operational checklist (for enabling canary in a new region)
1) Confirm control-plane config has:
   - canary enabled for region with a limited model set (2–3 to start)
   - QPS caps set to defaults (or lower)
   - emergency disable wired and tested
2) Confirm observability:
   - overhead metric present: `perf_canary_gpu_overhead_ratio` (or equivalent)
   - canary throttling metric present
   - dashboard panels load within acceptable latency
3) Confirm privacy/safety:
   - prompt redaction assertions enabled
   - log sampling at default
   - no high-cardinality labels added
4) Perform a 30–60 minute bake:
   - verify region overhead <= 0.5%
   - verify no increase in customer p95 attributable to canary
5) Enable warning alerts only; tune thresholds for 1–2 weeks before paging.

## Data retention and access
- Canary metrics follow standard performance metric retention (per telemetry policy). If histograms materially increase cost, downsample rather than add new labels.
- Canary logs are retained per default infra logging policy, but must remain low volume.
- Access to canary config and baseline artifacts must be limited to runtime/SRE/telemetry roles.

## Compliance and auditability
The following must be auditable:
- When/where canary is enabled (region/tier/model) and by whom.
- Emergency disable usage and duration.
- Any exception approvals.

## Appendix A: Capacity budget calculation (reference)
A typical budget implementation can be expressed as:
- Let `C_region_gpu_s` be total GPU-seconds provisioned per minute in targeted pools.
- Let `B_region = 0.005 * C_region_gpu_s` (0.5% budget).
- Let `E_req_gpu_s` be estimated GPU-seconds per request for a workload profile (use rolling estimate with conservative upper bound).
- Allow requests while `sum(E_req_gpu_s) <= B_region` over a rolling window.

Important:
- Use conservative estimates to avoid accidental budget overshoot.
- Budget must be enforced independently per region and (when possible) per SKU.

## Appendix B: Disallowed content examples
perf-canary must not emit any of the following:
- Raw prompt text or completion text
- Hashes/fingerprints of prompts that can be correlated back to customers
- Customer identifiers, org ids, project ids, IP addresses
- Request headers that could contain customer tokens

## Owners
- Standard owner: Observability/Alerting Standards (Allison Grant)
- Runtime implementation owner: Serving Runtime (Noah Patel)
- SRE escalation owner: SRE (Rafael Mendes)

## Revision history
- 2025-12-05: Initial publication (standards for prod rollout phase 1)
- 2026-02-15: Clarified burst allowance, added do-not-page conditions, tightened label restrictions after early false-positive/noise learnings

title: Perf Canary Overhead and Safety Standards
preview:
## Summary
This document defines mandatory overhead and safety standards for the production **perf-canary** service (the always-on synthetic performance canary used to detect serving-runtime regressions per model).

These standards exist to ensure:
- Canary load remains **non-disruptive** to customer workloads (bounded GPU capacity and QPS).
- Canary traffic is **isolated** (least privilege, no customer data access).
- Canary telemetry is **safe-by-default** (no prompts, no PII, controlled metric cardinality).
- The canary has clear **kill switches** and rollout controls to reduce blast radius.

## Scope
Applies to:
- The **perf-canary** service and any sidecars/jobs that generate synthetic inference traffic in production.
- Related telemetry (metrics, traces, logs) produced by canary runs.
- Control-plane configuration surfaces that enable/disable canary per region/tier/model.

Does not apply to:
- Customer-traffic shadowing or replay (explicitly out of scope for perf-canary MVP).
- Offline benchmarking jobs in staging/benchmark-lab (covered by separate performance test standards).

## Definitions
- **Covered model**: model included in canary coverage list for a region/tier.
- **Region**: Redwood production region (e.g., us-east, eu-west).
- **Tier**: capacity tier/pool class (Hosted, Dedicated, Private) or internal pool partition as defined by runtime control plane.
- **GPU overhead**: incremental GPU time/compute consumed by canary traffic, expressed as a % of provisioned capacity for the targeted pool(s).
- **Synthetic prompt**: shape-only prompt content generated by Redwood-owned templates. Must not include customer prompts or user identifiers.

## Hard requirements (must)
### R1: GPU overhead budgets
Perf-canary must remain under the following overhead budgets:
- **Per region (aggregate across all covered models): <= 0.5%** of provisioned GPU capacity for the region’s targeted pool(s).
- **Per model (within a region/tier): <= 0.10%** of provisioned GPU capacity for that model’s targeted pool(s).
- **Burst allowance (short-lived):** up to **1.0%** region aggregate for **<= 10 minutes** during controlled operations (e.g., baseline recomputation) with SRE approval and explicit change record.

Interpretation and measurement:
- Overhead is measured as GPU-seconds (or equivalent utilization proxy) attributable to canary requests vs total GPU-seconds in the targeted pool(s).
- If the canary runs across multiple SKUs in a region, overhead must be computed per SKU pool and then aggregated.

Automatic enforcement:
- perf-canary must enforce a **capacity budget** limiter. When the budget is exhausted, canary requests must back off (do not retry aggressively).
- If the overhead limiter fails closed (cannot compute budget), perf-canary must default to **safe minimum** operation (see R8).

### R2: QPS and concurrency caps
Perf-canary must enforce caps at three levels:
1) **Global (service-wide) cap** per region (default):
   - Max canary QPS (region): **0.2 QPS** per covered model, with a region-wide hard max of **2 QPS**.
2) **Per model cap** per region/tier (default):
   - Max canary QPS (model): **0.2 QPS**
   - Max in-flight requests (model): **2**
3) **Per endpoint cap** (safety net):
   - If the inference endpoint reports elevated errors/overload signals, canary must reduce QPS to **<= 0.02 QPS** and emit a “canary_throttled” metric.

Notes:
- Defaults above are intentionally conservative and can be tuned downward for small pools.
- Any increase above defaults requires SRE review + documented capacity impact estimate.

### R3: Traffic isolation and routing
Perf-canary traffic must be isolated so it does not compete unexpectedly with customer traffic:
- perf-canary must use a dedicated **service account / auth scope** ("internal.canary.inference.read") with least privilege.
- Requests must include a canary-only routing attribute (e.g., header `X-Redwood-Canary: perf`) so runtime can:
  - apply separate rate limits,
  - exclude canary traffic from customer billing/quotas,
  - optionally route to designated canary partitions when available.
- Canary traffic must not bypass normal admission control. If the system is overloaded, canary should back off.

### R4: No customer data; no prompt storage
Perf-canary must not collect, store, or emit customer data:
- Canary prompts must be **synthetic** and generated by Redwood-owned templates.
- Canary must not ingest prompts from production traffic, logs, traces, queues, dead-letter streams, or customer datasets.
- Canary must not log prompt text, completions, embeddings content, or tool/function arguments.

Allowed:
- Non-content metadata required for debugging (timestamps, model id, region, runtime build tag, kernel tag, sample counts).
- Token counts (input/output lengths) and latency/throughput measurements.

### R5: PII and secrets handling
Perf-canary must enforce and validate:
- Redaction on all logs (prompt-like fields must be dropped, not masked).
- Explicit assertions in code that prevent prompt payloads from reaching log/trace exporters.
- No API keys, bearer tokens, or customer identifiers in logs/metrics/traces.
- Service account credentials must be stored only in approved secret stores (no env var plaintext in manifests).

### R6: Metric label cardinality limits
To prevent metric explosions and alert storms:
- Metric dimensions must be limited to:
  - `model`, `model_version`, `region`, `tier`, `hardware_sku`, `runtime_build_id`, `kernel_tag` (when available), `workload_profile` (small fixed set)
- Explicitly disallowed labels:
  - `request_id`, `trace_id`, `pod`, `node`, `gpu_uuid`, `customer_id`, `prompt_hash`, any free-form string.
- `model_version` must use canonical registry IDs (not arbitrary strings).

Any new label requires telemetry platform review (Logan Wright / Nadia Rahman or delegate).

### R7: Safe defaults for traces and logs
- Tracing sampling for perf-canary must be **low by default** (e.g., 0.1–1%) and must never capture request/response content.
- Logs must be structured and limited to warnings/errors + periodic summary lines (e.g., every 5 minutes).
- Debug logging that increases verbosity must be gated behind a runtime flag and time-limited.

### R8: Kill switches and fail-safe behavior
Perf-canary must support:
- **Control-plane disable switch** per region/tier/model (audited, with last-changed metadata).
- **Emergency global disable** (single toggle) that stops request generation within **60 seconds**.

Fail-safe behavior:
- If baseline store is unavailable, telemetry is delayed, or capacity budget computation fails, canary must:
  - reduce QPS to **<= 0.02 QPS per model** (or fully disable if configured), and
  - emit a single warning alert (non-paging) indicating degraded canary mode.

### R9: Do-not-page conditions (protect on-call)
perf-canary must suppress paging in these conditions (warn-only):
- Known telemetry ingestion delays beyond threshold (e.g., >10 minutes lag).
- Canary sample size below minimum thresholds for the window.
- Ongoing region incident or declared capacity event (as signaled by SRE incident state).

### R10: Change management and exceptions
- Any configuration change that increases load (QPS/concurrency/workload size) must be documented with:
  - estimated incremental overhead (%),
  - expected affected pools (region/tier/SKU),
  - rollback plan.
- Exceptions require approval from:
  - Serving Runtime TL (Noah Patel or delegate) AND
  - SRE sponsor (Sean Gallagher or delegate)

Exceptions must be time-bounded and tracked as a follow-up task.

## Recommended targets (should)
### S1: Workload profiles and run cadence
- Use a **small fixed set** of workload profiles (e.g., `short_prefill`, `medium_chat`, `long_prefill`, `decode_heavy`) to avoid config drift.
- Run cadence should be steady (avoid synchronized spikes at minute boundaries). Use jitter (e.g., +/- 20%).

### S2: Pool-aware backoff
- If runtime reports elevated queueing time or overload signals, canary should:
  - reduce QPS,
  - increase jitter,
  - optionally skip the heaviest prompt bucket.

### S3: Baseline recomputation discipline
- Baseline recomputation (if done in production-like environments) should be scheduled during lower-risk windows and must honor burst allowance rules.

## Operational checklist (for enabling canary in a new region)
1) Confirm control-plane config has:
   - canary enabled for region with a limited model set (2–3 to start)
   - QPS caps set to defaults (or lower)
   - emergency disable wired and tested
2) Confirm observability:
   - overhead metric present: `perf_canary_gpu_overhead_ratio` (or equivalent)
   - canary throttling metric present
   - dashboard panels load within acceptable latency
3) Confirm privacy/safety:
   - prompt redaction assertions enabled
   - log sampling at default
   - no high-cardinality labels added
4) Perform a 30–60 minute bake:
   - verify region overhead <= 0.5%
   - verify no increase in customer p95 attributable to canary
5) Enable warning alerts only; tune thresholds for 1–2 weeks before paging.

## Data retention and access
- Canary metrics follow standard performance metric retention (per telemetry policy). If histograms materially increase cost, downsample rather than add new labels.
- Canary logs are retained per default infra logging policy, but must remain low volume.
- Access to canary config and baseline artifacts must be limited to runtime/SRE/telemetry roles.

## Compliance and auditability
The following must be auditable:
- When/where canary is enabled (region/tier/model) and by whom.
- Emergency disable usage and duration.
- Any exception approvals.

## Appendix A: Capacity budget calculation (reference)
A typical budget implementation can be expressed as:
- Let `C_region_gpu_s` be total GPU-seconds provisioned per minute in targeted pools.
- Let `B_region = 0.005 * C_region_gpu_s` (0.5% budget).
- Let `E_req_gpu_s` be estimated GPU-seconds per request for a workload profile (use rolling estimate with conservative upper bound).
- Allow requests while `sum(E_req_gpu_s) <= B_region` over a rolling window.

Important:
- Use conservative estimates to avoid accidental budget overshoot.
- Budget must be enforced independently per region and (when possible) per SKU.

## Appendix B: Disallowed content examples
perf-canary must not emit any of the following:
- Raw prompt text or completion text
- Hashes/fingerprints of prompts that can be correlated back to customers
- Customer identifiers, org ids, project ids, IP addresses
- Request headers that could contain customer tokens

## Owners
- Standard owner: Observability/Alerting Standards (Allison Grant)
- Runtime implementation owner: Serving Runtime (Noah Patel)
- SRE escalation owner: SRE (Rafael Mendes)

## Revision history
- 2025-12-05: Initial publication (standards for prod rollout phase 1)
- 2026-02-15: Clarified burst allowance, added do-not-page conditions, tightened label restrictions after early false-positive/noise learnings
